tasks.chapterTitle: Oxygen Therapy, Abdominal, Musculoskeletal, and Integumentary Assessment
Medication Administration, Tube Management, Specimen Collection, Wound Care, Elimination, Tracheostomy, and IV Therapy

messages.content:
### Summary: *Nursing Skills - 2e* (Open RN)

This textbook is an open educational resource (OER) designed for prelicensure nursing students, including practical/vocational programs and first-year registered nursing cohorts. It provides evidence-based instruction on fundamental clinical skills and physical assessment techniques [S.19].

#### **Key Objectives and Content**
*   **Clinical Skills Training:** The text covers physical assessment across the lifespan, mathematical calculations for medication administration, and various procedural skills (e.g., wound care, oxygen therapy, specimen collection, and intravenous therapy) [S.19, S.20].
*   **Standards Alignment:** The resource is updated to reflect the **2023 NCLEX-PN and NCLEX-RN Test Plans** and incorporates "Next-Generation" learning activities to foster clinical judgment [S.19, S.20].
*   **Evidence-Based Approach:** Instruction is structured around the nursing process, featuring body-systems assessment, sample documentation, and detailed skill checklists for consistent clinical practice [S.19, S.20].

#### **Structure and Resources**
*   **Comprehensive Modules:** The text is divided into 23 chapters covering areas including the General Survey, Health History, Aseptic Technique, Neurological/Physical Assessments, and various Medication Administration routes [S.5–S.16].
*   **Interactive Learning:** The online version includes embedded interactive activities and supplementary demonstration videos, which are essential for practical skill development [S.19, S.20].
*   **Open Access:** The book is licensed under **Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC-BY 4.0)**, allowing users to share and adapt the content. It is available in multiple formats, including a free printable PDF and an affordable print version via XanEdu [S.19, S.20, S.29].

#### **Development and Peer Review**
The content was developed through a collaborative effort involving Chippewa Valley Technical College and various nursing faculty, with extensive peer review to ensure accuracy and clinical applicability across the nursing curriculum [S.22, S.25].

Lesson 2 — Nursing Skills - Presentation9 slides
Nursing Skills: Hands-on Procedures
  • Foundational skills for clinical nursing practice.
  • Comprehensive resource for nursing students.

The *Nursing Skills (2nd Edition)* textbook is an open educational resource (OER) developed by Chippewa Valley Technical College under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License, supported by a Department of Education grant [S.4, S.19].

### Core Purpose and Scope
*   **Target Audience:** Designed for prelicensure nursing students, including those in practical/vocational programs and first-year registered nurse programs [S.19].
*   **Educational Foundation:** The content centers on evidence-based clinical skills and physical assessments across the lifespan, aligned with the 2023 NCLEX-PN and NCLEX-RN test plans and the Wisconsin Technical College System curriculum [S.19, S.20].
*   **Skill Development:** The text covers a wide array of competencies, including health history collection, physical assessment (body systems approach), aseptic technique, mathematical calculations (e.g., dosage formulas), medication administration, and various specialized clinical procedures like wound care and tracheostomy management [S.5 through S.16].

### Learning Features
*   **Clinical Judgment:** In accordance with the NCSBN Clinical Judgment Measurement Model, the second edition incorporates NCLEX Next-Generation style questions with immediate formative feedback [S.20].
*   **Practical Tools:** Each chapter provides skills checklists, sample documentation, and supplementary demonstration videos to support clinical performance [S.19, S.20].
*   **Accessibility:** The text is available for free online, as a downloadable PDF, or as an affordable print version. The online format is optimized for interactive learning activities [S.20, S.21].

### Quantitative Competency
The text integrates essential clinical math, utilizing methods such as dimensional analysis (e.g., for conversion and infusion calculations) to ensure patient safety [S.7]. For instance, it provides guidance for calculating intravenous infusions by pump:
$$ \text{Infusion Rate (mL/hr)} = \frac{\text{Volume (mL)}}{\text{Time (hr)}} $$

This document serves as the preface and front matter for the second edition of the *Open RN Nursing Skills* textbook, developed by Chippewa Valley Technical College.

### Purpose and Development
The textbook was created through the collaborative efforts of faculty from the Wisconsin Technical College System (WTCS) to provide an evidence-based, open educational resource (OER) for nursing students. The content was developed by a team of authors and contributors, reviewed by peer experts, and guided by an advisory committee of clinical and academic professionals.

### Licensing and Usage
The material is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International ($CC\ BY\ 4.0$) license. This allows users to share and adapt the content for any purpose, provided that they:
* Provide appropriate credit to the authors.
* Include a link to the license.
* Indicate if any modifications were made to the original work.

### Standards and Conceptual Framework
The textbook is designed to align with essential professional nursing standards and competencies, including:
* American Nurses Association ($ANA$) Standards of Practice and Code of Ethics.
* National Council Licensure Examination ($NCLEX$) test plans for RNs and PNs.
* Quality and Safety Education for Nurses ($QSEN$) competencies.
* National League of Nursing ($NLN$) graduate competencies.
* American Association of Colleges of Nursing ($AACN$) Essentials.
* Guidelines from Healthy People 2030 ($[S.33]$).

### Pedagogical Approach
The curriculum incorporates a conceptual framework focused on:
* **Holism:** Addressing the interrelationship between patients, their environment, and their developmental, cultural, and spiritual needs ($[S.33]$).
* **Evidence-Based Practice:** Utilizing current, academically rigorous references and providing digital access to credible resources ($[S.33]$).
* **Patient Safety:** Highlighting both expected and unexpected clinical findings to prepare students for rapid assessment and reporting ($[S.34]$).
* **Inclusivity:** Employing clear, accessible language and gender-neutral pronouns to support diverse student populations and inclusive clinical care ($[S.34]$).

**Summary of Chapter 2: Health History (pp. 99-154)**

This chapter provides a comprehensive framework for conducting a systematic health history, which serves as a foundational component of patient assessment.

**Key Components of a Health History**
The health history process is designed to gather vital patient information through structured inquiry:
- Demographic and Biological Data: Basic patient identification and biological context (p. 105).
- Reason for Seeking Health Care: Establishing the patient's primary concern or chief complaint (p. 110).
- Medical History: Documenting both current conditions and past medical history (p. 116).
- Family Health History: Identifying genetic predispositions and familial health trends (p. 119).
- Functional Health and ADLs: Evaluating the patient's ability to perform Activities of Daily Living, which indicates their level of independence (p. 120).
- Review of Body Systems: A systematic review to identify subjective symptoms across all physiological systems (p. 142).

**Practical Application and Documentation**
The chapter emphasizes the necessity of consistent documentation to ensure accurate communication among healthcare team members (p. 143). Students are guided through the practical application of these concepts via specific checklists designed to standardize the interview process (p. 144) and supplementary tools, such as sample health history forms (p. 152). Learning activities are provided to reinforce the integration of these data points into clinical practice (p. 146).

Part III (pages 155-182) covers the essential principles and procedures for blood pressure assessment in nursing practice. The key points are summarized below:

Blood Pressure Introduction and Basics
The section establishes the importance of blood pressure monitoring as a foundational vital sign. It defines basic concepts related to hemodynamics and the physiological factors that influence readings, providing the theoretical background necessary for accurate clinical measurement (p. 155, 160).

Blood Pressure Assessment
This portion details the clinical procedure for assessing blood pressure. It emphasizes the importance of following standardized techniques to ensure accuracy, including proper cuff sizing, patient positioning, and the correct application of auscultatory methods to detect Korotkoff sounds (p. 156).

Documentation and Systematic Checklists
To ensure clinical competence and accuracy in reporting, the text provides structured workflows. A dedicated checklist is included to guide students through the manual blood pressure measurement process step-by-step (p. 172). Additionally, the section demonstrates how to record findings correctly in a patient’s health record to maintain clear and professional documentation (p. 171).

Practical Resources
The chapter rounds out the learning experience by offering supplementary video resources for visual learners (p. 175) and various learning activities designed to reinforce the comprehension of measurement techniques and data interpretation (p. 176). A glossary is provided at the end of the section to clarify key terminology used throughout the chapter (p. 179).

Part IV of the text (pp. 183-250) focuses on Aseptic Technique, which is essential to nursing practice for preventing the transmission of microorganisms. The key concepts are summarized below:

Aseptic Technique Fundamentals
This section defines the basic principles of asepsis, which involves practices designed to maintain a clean environment and prevent the spread of infection. It emphasizes the importance of following standardized protocols to minimize the risk of healthcare-associated infections.

Sterile Fields and Equipment
A significant portion of this section details the creation and maintenance of sterile fields. Maintaining a sterile field requires meticulous attention to technique to ensure that sterile items remain uncontaminated. This includes the specific procedures for applying and removing sterile gloves, as improper technique can easily compromise both the nurse and the patient.

Hand Hygiene Protocols
Hand hygiene is identified as the most effective method to reduce the transmission of pathogens. The content covers two primary methods:
1. Hand hygiene with soap and water, which is required when hands are visibly soiled or when caring for patients with specific infections like Clostridioides difficile.
2. Hand hygiene with alcohol-based hand sanitizer, which is the preferred method for routine decontamination when hands are not visibly soiled.

Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
The text outlines the requirements for using PPE, which acts as a barrier between the nurse and potentially infectious materials. Proper application and removal of PPE are scrutinized to ensure that contaminants are not transferred to the skin or clothing during the process.

Practical Application
The chapter provides detailed checklists for these procedures, serving as a resource for clinical competency. The goal is to reinforce evidence-based habits, such as identifying when to switch between sterile and clean techniques depending on the task being performed.

Part V, comprising pages 251 through 328, provides a comprehensive guide to nursing math calculations required for safe clinical practice. The key points are summarized below:

**Foundational Concepts**
The chapter establishes the necessity of accuracy in medication administration. It covers essential prerequisites, including the use of military time to prevent errors, standard equivalencies across measurement systems, and precise rules for rounding numerical answers to ensure patient safety (p. 251, 253, 259, 262, 267).

**Calculation Methodologies**
The text emphasizes the use of dimensional analysis as a primary method for solving complex dosage problems. This is applied to various scenarios, including:
* Tablet and liquid medication concentrations (p. 269, 276, 281).
* Reconstituted medications and weight-based dosing calculations (p. 286, 293).
* Verifying that prescribed dosages fall within a safe dosage range for the patient (p. 298).

**Intravenous (IV) Therapy Calculations**
A significant portion of the section is dedicated to IV management, which is critical for patient safety in acute care settings. The material details:
* The calculation of infusion rates for both gravity-fed and electronic pump-controlled delivery systems (p. 301, 302, 310).
* Determining the total infusion time required to complete a prescribed volume (p. 316).
* Approaches for multi-step calculations that integrate several variables (p. 320).

**Key Mathematical Formulas**
The section relies on fundamental algebraic relationships. For example, when calculating the flow rate for an IV pump in milliliters per hour, the following logic applies:
$$ \text{Rate (mL/hr)} = \frac{\text{Total Volume (mL)}}{\text{Time (hr)}} $$

For gravity infusions measured in drops per minute, the formula incorporates the drop factor (gtt/mL) of the manual tubing:
$$ \text{Rate (gtt/min)} = \frac{\text{Volume (mL)} \times \text{Drop Factor (gtt/mL)}}{\text{Time (min)}} $$

This summary covers the assessment chapters for the Neurological (Part VI) and Head and Neck (Part VII) systems.

### Neurological Assessment (pp. 329–432)
*   **Fundamental Concepts:** The chapter establishes the basics of the nervous system, focusing on the integration of mental status, cranial nerve function, and physical capabilities.
*   **Systematic Evaluation:**
    *   **Mental Status:** Assessment of orientation, cognition, and awareness.
    *   **Cranial Nerves:** Systematic testing of the twelve cranial nerves to evaluate sensory and motor function in the face and head.
    *   **Sensory and Motor:** Evaluation includes checking muscle strength, sensory perceptions (light touch, pain), and cerebellar function (coordination and balance).
    *   **Reflexes:** Assessing deep tendon reflexes to determine the integrity of the reflex arc.
*   **Documentation:** Emphasis is placed on standardized reporting of neurological findings to track patient status and identify changes.

### Head and Neck Assessment (pp. 433–492)
*   **Anatomical Review:** Focuses on the structures of the cranium, face, eyes, ears, nose, mouth, and throat.
*   **Assessment Components:**
    *   **Physical Inspection:** Examining the symmetry, skin integrity, and structure of facial features.
    *   **Palpation:** Assessment of the scalp, skull, and the thyroid gland for masses or abnormal textures.
    *   **Common Conditions:** Review of expected versus unexpected findings, identifying common pathological conditions found during clinical observation.
*   **Clinical Proficiency:** The section provides specific checklists and supplementary video resources to assist in performing physical assessments, ensuring that findings are documented accurately according to established clinical standards.

Both chapters prioritize a structured approach to care, moving from basic physiological concepts to actionable nursing assessments and documentation requirements.

This section covers the clinical assessment of the eyes, ears, cardiovascular system, and respiratory system.

**Eye and Ear Assessment (pp. 493–536)**
This chapter focuses on the basic anatomical and physiological concepts required to evaluate sensory organs. It provides a structured approach to assessing visual and auditory function, including clinical checklists for examinations, standard documentation practices, and learning activities to reinforce diagnostic skills.

**Cardiovascular Assessment (pp. 541–588)**
The cardiovascular assessment section details the examination of the heart and circulatory system. It covers the foundational knowledge needed to assess heart sounds, pulses, and perfusion. The content includes assessment techniques, sample documentation for findings, and instructional checklists to ensure consistent nursing practice and data collection.

**Respiratory Assessment (pp. 593–627)**
The final portion of this area addresses the respiratory system. It explores the physiological basics of breathing and gas exchange, providing a systematic approach to assessing lung sounds and respiratory effort. It concludes with specific assessment checklists, documentation guidelines, and supplemental resources to assist in identifying both expected and unexpected respiratory findings.

The provided document covers comprehensive nursing procedures ranging from oxygen therapy to musculoskeletal assessment. Below is a summary of the key subject areas between internal page references 633 and 834.

**Oxygen Therapy (Part XI, p. 633–692)**
This section focuses on the principles of oxygenation, the various types of equipment used to deliver oxygen, and the application of the nursing process. It emphasizes proper assessment to ensure patient safety and effective oxygen delivery, supported by clinical checklists and documentation protocols.

**Abdominal Assessment (Part XII, p. 697–724)**
This content details the clinical examination of the gastrointestinal and genitourinary systems. It covers foundational anatomical knowledge and the systematic assessment techniques required to evaluate these body systems, including standardized documentation and observation checklists.

**Musculoskeletal Assessment (Part XIII, p. 729–781)**
This section outlines the assessment of the musculoskeletal system, including the evaluation of muscles, bones, and joints. It provides an overview of common physiological conditions, assessment methodology, and the necessary clinical steps for documenting musculoskeletal health.

**Integumentary Assessment (Part XIV, p. 787–830)**
This part focuses on the assessment of the skin, hair, and nails. It covers basic integumentary concepts, the identification of common skin conditions, and the systematic process for performing a comprehensive skin assessment.

**Administration of Enteral Medications (Part XV, p. 835–834)**
This section begins the coverage of medication administration, specifically focusing on the enteral route. It includes foundational concepts of safe drug administration, the specific assessments required prior to administering medications, and checklists for oral, rectal, and enteral tube administration to ensure standardized and safe practice.

This section of the text covers advanced clinical skills, medication administration, and specialized patient care procedures.

### Medication Administration
- **Enteral and Other Routes:** Chapters 15 and 16 detail the administration of enteral medications (oral, rectal, and tube-based) and other routes such as transdermal, ophthalmic, otic, inhalation, and vaginal applications.
- **Parenteral Medications:** Chapter 18 focuses on injections, including intradermal, subcutaneous, and intramuscular administration, emphasizing evidence-based practices and the nursing process.
- **Calculations:** Chapter 5 provides foundational mathematical skills for nurses, covering conversions, weight-based calculations ($ \text{Dosage} = \text{Desired} / \text{Have} \times \text{Volume} $), safe dosage ranges, and intravenous infusion management (gravity and pump).

### Specialized Clinical Procedures
- **Enteral Tube Management:** Chapter 17 covers the placement and management of nasal-gastric (NG) tubes, including suction and irrigation.
- **Specimen Collection:** Chapter 19 outlines protocols for collecting various specimens, such as blood glucose, nasal/oropharyngeal swabs, sputum, and stool.
- **Wound Care:** Chapter 20 addresses the assessment of wounds and the application of therapies, including dressing changes, irrigation, packing, and the removal of sutures or staples.
- **Elimination Facilitation:** Chapter 21 provides guidelines for urinary catheterization (both insertion and removal), prevention of CAUTIs, and ostomy management.

### Assessment and Documentation
Across these chapters, the manual prioritizes:
- Using standardized **checklists** to ensure safety during invasive procedures.
- Applying the **nursing process** systematically to medication administration, tube management, and patient care.
- Proper **documentation** techniques for all monitored clinical activities and assessments.
Here is a quiz for this lesson.

questions.question: The Open RN Nursing Skills textbook is designed to be used by which of the following groups?
options: Only advanced registered nurses seeking specialization.
Prelicensure nursing students in practical/vocational or first-year programs.
Medical doctors and surgical residents.
Allied health professionals such as physical therapists and dietitians.
questions.explanation: The textbook is specifically developed for prelicensure nursing students, including those in practical/vocational programs and first-year courses for registered nurses.

questions.question: What is the primary focus of the Open RN Nursing Skills textbook?
options: The development of clinical skills and physical assessment routinely performed by entry-level nurses.
Advanced surgical techniques and complex internal medicine procedures.
Management of hospital administrative departments and nursing floor logistics.
Theoretical research regarding the history and evolution of nursing education.
questions.explanation: The text focuses on evidence-based clinical skills and physical assessments that are routinely performed by entry-level nurses across the life span.

questions.question: How are the learning activities in the second edition of the textbook designed to support student growth?
options: By requiring students to memorize technical medical terminology.
By utilizing NCLEX Next-Generation style questions to encourage clinical judgment.
By focusing exclusively on written examination preparation for state boards.
By providing only static, non-interactive printable assessments.
questions.explanation: The second edition includes NCLEX Next-Generation style questions that provide formative feedback to help students develop clinical judgment based on the NCSBN Clinical Judgment Measurement Model.

questions.question: Which of the following topics is included as a distinct part within the Nursing Skills textbook?
options: Advanced Cardiovascular Surgical Procedures.
Psychiatric Inpatient Facility Policy.
Math Calculations for clinical applications.
Public Health Policy and Lobbying.
questions.explanation: Chapter 5 of the textbook is dedicated to Math Calculations, covering topics such as conversions, dosage, and IV infusions, which are essential for clinical practice.

questions.question: Why is the online version of the Open RN textbook identified as essential?
options: It is the only version that contains accurate information.
It allows for interaction with the learning activities included in each chapter.
The print version is restricted by copyright to faculty members only.
The online version is the only one that includes the glossary.
questions.explanation: The online version is required for students to interact with the learning activities, such as simulation-based exercises, that are embedded within the digital content.

cards.front: What is the primary focus of the Open RN 'Nursing Skills' textbook?
cards.back: The development of evidence-based clinical skills and physical assessment across the life span routinely performed by entry-level nurses.

cards.front: According to the text, which organization's test plans are the 2nd edition content aligned with?
cards.back: The 2023 NCLEX-PN and NCLEX-RN Test Plans.

cards.front: What does 'Aseptic Technique' cover in the context of nursing skills?
cards.back: Aseptic technique covers basic concepts, sterile field creation, and specific checklists for hand hygiene, PPE, and applying/removing sterile gloves.

cards.front: What are the common components involved in obtaining a 'Health History'?
cards.back: Components include demographic/biological data, reason for seeking care, current and past medical history, family health history, functional health/ADLs, and review of body systems.

cards.front: Which specific nursing chapters focus on the 'Assessment' of patients?
cards.back: Chapters 6-10 and 12-14, which cover Neurological, Head and Neck, Eye and Ear, Cardiovascular, Respiratory, Abdominal, Musculoskeletal, and Integumentary assessments.

cards.front: What does the textbook include to help students verify their understanding of physical assessment techniques?
cards.back: The textbook includes sample skills checklists and instructor demonstration videos for assessment procedures.

cards.front: How does the 'Math Calculations' chapter assist nursing students?
cards.back: It covers clinical math including military time, conversions, tablet dosage, liquid concentrations, weight-based calculations, IV infusion calculations, and safe dosage ranges.

cards.front: What type of clinical judgment model is emphasized in the NCLEX Next-Generation learning activities?
cards.back: The NCSBN Clinical Judgment Measurement Model.

cards.front: What is covered under 'Parenteral Medication Administration' in Chapter 18?
cards.back: Evidence-based practices for injections, including intradermal, subcutaneous, and intramuscular administration techniques.

cards.front: What is the difference between the 'Nursing Skills' textbook and the 'Nursing Advanced Skills' textbook?
cards.back: The 'Nursing Skills' textbook focuses on entry-level skills, while 'Nursing Advanced Skills' covers topics like IV infusion, blood product administration, central lines, chest tubes, and ECG interpretation.

slides.title: Nursing Skills: Hands-on Procedures
bullets: Foundational skills for clinical nursing practice.
slides.notes: This presentation explores the essential practical skills needed for patient care in a clinical setting based on the Open RN nursing curriculum.

slides.title: General Survey
slides.notes: The general survey is our primary assessment tool, focusing on the patient's immediate status through observation and vital sign measurement.

slides.title: Health History
bullets: Identifying patients' reason for seeking care (p. 110).
Documenting past medical and family history (p. 116).
slides.notes: A thorough health history provides the narrative context for the patient's current clinical situation, guiding our future interventions.

slides.title: Blood Pressure Assessment
slides.notes: Accurate blood pressure reading is a critical nursing skill that requires mastery of manual measurement protocols to ensure patient safety.

slides.title: Aseptic Technique
bullets: Proper use of personal protective equipment (p. 238).
slides.notes: Aseptic technique is the bedrock of clinical safety, preventing cross-contamination and protecting both the patient and the healthcare provider.

slides.title: Math Calculations
bullets: Dimensional analysis and safe dosage verification (p. 269).
Intravenous infusion and gravity flow pumps (p. 301).
slides.notes: Precision in medication math is essential—these calculations ensure the nurse delivers the exact, safe dose prescribed by clinical providers.

slides.title: Neuromuscular & Head/Neck Assessment
bullets: Assessing mental status and cranial nerves (p. 350).
Evaluating sensory function and motor strength (p. 381).
Reflex testing and cerebellar function exams (p. 389).
Head, neck, and associated clinical assessments (p. 433).
slides.notes: These assessments provide a comprehensive neurological and physical overview, helping identify specific regional issues during clinical examination.

slides.title: Sensory & Vital System Assessments
bullets: Evaluating eye, ear, cardiovascular, and respiratory systems (p. 493).
Musculoskeletal status and physical movement analysis (p. 764).
Integrated checklists for systematic patient review (p. 620).
slides.notes: System-specific assessments allow the nurse to focus on vital functions, ensuring that cardiovascular, respiratory, and musculoskeletal health are properly maintained.

slides.title: Advanced Clinical Procedures
bullets: Managing IV therapy and tracheostomy care (p. 835).
slides.notes: This section covers complex clinical interventions required for acute and chronic care management, utilizing high-level nursing protocols.
